answer: \boxed{Diane will know the hit man has killed Camilla when she receives the **blue key**—the same one mentioned in the hit man’s cryptic instructions—indicating the job is complete. The key’s significance is tied to the narrative’s recurring motifs of identity and revelation, suggesting it unlocks confirmation of Camilla’s fate. Additionally, the hit man’s promise of the key implies it will serve as a tangible, eerie "proof" of the assassination.}
